title: Reset Edit Mode When Navigating Between Metrics
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want edit mode to automatically reset to read-only when I navigate to another metric using Previous/Next arrows, so that unsaved changes are clearly discarded and I start fresh with each metric.
description:
When the sidebar is in edit mode and the user clicks Previous or Next to navigate to another metric, the sidebar should automatically switch back to read-only (output) mode and refresh all data for the newly selected metric. This prevents confusion where edit-mode fields from one metric carry over visually to another.

The fix also ensures that Reports and Dashboards tabs refresh correctly when navigating between metrics — previously they would show stale data from the prior metric.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking Previous/Next while in edit mode automatically resets to read-only mode
All metric detail fields refresh with data from the newly selected metric
Reports tab refreshes to show reports linked to the new metric
Dashboards tab refreshes to show dashboards linked to the new metric
Unsaved edits are discarded without a save prompt on navigation
ux_changes: Previous/Next navigation resets the sidebar to read-only mode. All tabs (detail, reports, dashboards) refresh with the new metric's data. Edit mode state is cleared on navigation.
implementation_notes: Navigation handlers now call the edit-mode reset function before loading the new metric's data. Reports and dashboards tab data is also refreshed during navigation. Implemented in 45 minutes.
comment_summary: Issue clarified via a recorded screencast video. Developer fixed navigation to reset edit mode and refresh all tabs. Reports and dashboards stale data issue also resolved in the same fix.
